volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Prometheus Bound (Section 840 - Section 986)

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: σαφῶς ἐπίστασ , Ἰόνιος κεκλήσεται , [*]
sections.translation:
be well assured , shall bear the name Ionian , as a memorial of your crossing for all mankind .
These , then , are the tokens to you of my understanding , to show that it discerns more than has been made manifest . The rest I shall declare both to you and her ,

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: ἐς ταὐτὸν ἐλθὼν τῶν πάλαι λόγων ἴχνος .
sections.translation: returning to the track of my former tale .

sections.label: Section 846
sections.original: ἔστιν πόλις Κάνωβος ἐσχάτη χθονός ,
sections.translation: There is a city , Canobus , on the extremity of the land at the very mouth and silt-bar of the Nile . There at last Zeus restores you to your senses by the mere stroke and touch of his unterrifying hand .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: ἐπώνυμον δὲ τῶν Διὸς γεννημάτων
sections.translation: And you shall bring forth dark Epaphus , [*] thus named from the manner of Zeus ’ engendering ; and he shall gather the fruit of all the land watered by the broad-flowing Nile . Fifth in descent from him , fifty maidens shall return to Argos , not of their own

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: θηλύσπορος , φεύγουσα συγγενῆ γάμον
sections.translation: free choice , but fleeing marriage with their cousin kin ; while these , their hearts ablaze with passion , like falcons eagerly pursuing doves , shall come in pursuit of wedlock unlawful to pursue ; but God shall grudge them enjoyment of their brides .

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: Πελασγία δὲ δέξεται θηλυκτόνῳ
sections.translation: Pelasgian soil shall offer the maids a home , when , in the watches of the night , their husbands have been slain by a deed of daring wrought by the women ’ s murderous blows . For each bride shall take the life of her lord , dyeing a two-edged sword in his blood — in such ways may Love come upon my enemies !

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: μίαν δὲ παίδων ἵμερος θέλξει τὸ μὴ
sections.translation: However , love ’ s desire shall charm one of the maidens not to slay her mate ; rather , her resolve will lose its edge ; for she will make her choice between two evil names to be called coward rather than murderess . She it is who shall give birth in Argos to a royal line —

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: μακροῦ λόγου δεῖ ταῦτ ἐπεξελθεῖν τορῶς .
sections.translation: a long story is necessary to explain this clearly ; of her seed , however , shall be born a man of daring , renowned with the bow , who shall deliver me from these toils . [*] Such is the oracle recounted to me by my mother , Titan Themis , born long ago .

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: ὅπως δὲ χὤπη , ταῦτα δεῖ μακροῦ χρόνου
sections.translation: The manner and the means — these need lengthy speech to tell , and to learn them all would not be of any benefit .

sections.label: Section 877
sections.original: ἐλελεῦ , [*] ἐλελεῦ ,
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Alas ! Once again convulsive pain and frenzy , striking my brain , inflame me . I am stung by the gadfly ’ s barb ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: χρίει μ ἄπυρος :
sections.translation: unforged by fire . My heart knocks at my ribs in terror ; my eyeballs roll wildly round and round . I am carried out of my course by a fierce blast of madness ; I ’ ve lost all mastery over my tongue ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: θολεροὶ δὲ λόγοι παίουσ [*] εἰκῆ
sections.translation: and a stream of turbid words beats recklessly against the billows of dark destruction . Exit

sections.label: Section 1.887
sections.original: ἦ σοφὸς ἦ σοφὸς [ ἦν ] [*] ὃς
sections.translation: Ah , sage , sage indeed , was he who first pondered this truth in his mind and with his tongue gave it utterance —

sections.label: Section 1.890
sections.original: ὡς τὸ κηδεῦσαι καθ ἑαυτὸν ἀριστεύει μακρῷ ,
sections.translation: that to marry in one ’ s own class is far the best — a poor man should not desire to marry among those who are pampered by riches , or who are mighty in pride of birth .

sections.label: Section 1.894
sections.original: μήποτε μήποτέ μ , ὦ
sections.translation: Never , oh never ,

sections.label: Section 1.895
sections.original: πότνιαι [*] Μοῖραι , λεχέων Διὸς εὐνά-
sections.translation: immortal Fates , may you see me the partner of the bed of Zeus , and may I be wedded to no bridegroom who descends to me from heaven . For I shudder when I behold the loveless maidenhood of Io , cruelly crushed like this

sections.label: Section 1.900
sections.original: δυσπλάνοις [*] Ἥρας ἀλατείαις πόνων .
sections.translation: by her toilsome wanderings sent by Hera .

sections.label: Section 901
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δ ὅτε [*] μὲν ὁμαλὸς ὁ γάμος , [*]
sections.translation: When marriage is on equal terms , in my opinion it is no cause for dread ; so never may the love of the mightier gods cast on me its irresistible glance . That would indeed be a war that cannot be fought , a source of resourceless misery ; and

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: οὐδ ἔχω τίς ἂν γενοίμαν : τὰν Διὸς γὰρ οὐχ ὁρῶ
sections.translation: I do not know what would be my fate , for I do not see how I could escape the designs of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 907
sections.original: ἦ μὴν ἔτι Ζεύς , καίπερ αὐθάδη φρονῶν , [*]
sections.translation: Yes , truly , the day will come when Zeus , although stubborn of soul , shall be humbled , seeing that he plans a marriage that shall

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: θρόνων τ [*] ἄιστον ἐκβαλεῖ : πατρὸς δ [*] ἀρὰ
sections.translation: hurl him into oblivion from his sovereignty and throne ; and then immediately the curse his father Cronus invoked as he fell from his ancient throne , shall be fulfilled to the uttermost . Deliverance from such ruin no one of the gods can show him clearly except me .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: ἐγὼ τάδ οἶδα χᾦ τρόπῳ . πρὸς ταῦτά νυν [*]
sections.translation: I know the fact and the means . So let him sit there in his assurance , putting his trust in the crash reverberating on high and brandishing his fire-breathing bolt in his hands . For these shall not protect him from falling in ignominious and unendurable ruin .

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: τοῖον παλαιστὴν νῦν παρασκευάζεται
sections.translation: Such an adversary is he now preparing despite himself , a prodigy irresistible , even one who shall discover a flame mightier than the lightning and a deafening crash to outroar the thunder ; a prodigy who shall shiver the trident ,

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: τρίαιναν , αἰχμὴν τὴν Ποσειδῶνος , σκεδᾷ .
sections.translation: Poseidon ’ s spear , that scourge of the sea and shaker of the land . [*] Then , wrecked upon this evil , Zeus shall learn how different it is to be a sovereign and a slave .

sections.label: Section 928
sections.original: σύ θην [*] ἃ χρῄζεις , ταῦτ ἐπιγλωσσᾷ Διός .
sections.translation: Surely , it is only your own desire that you utter as a curse against Zeus .

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: ἅπερ τελεῖται , πρὸς δ ἃ βούλομαι λέγω .
sections.translation: I speak what shall be brought to pass and , moreover , my own desire .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: καὶ προσδοκᾶν χρὴ δεσπόσειν Ζηνός τινα ;
sections.translation: Must we really look for one to gain mastery over Zeus ?

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: καὶ τῶνδέ γ ἕξει δυσλοφωτέρους πόνους .
sections.translation: Yes , and he shall bear upon his neck pangs more galling than these of mine .

sections.label: Section 932
sections.original: πῶς δ [*] οὐχὶ ταρβεῖς τοιάδ ἐκρίπτων ἔπη ;
sections.translation: How is it that you are not afraid to utter such taunts ?

sections.label: Section 933
sections.original: τί δ ἂν [*] φοβοίμην ᾧ θανεῖν οὐ μόρσιμον ;
sections.translation: Why should I fear since I am fated not to die ?

sections.label: Section 934
sections.original: ἀλλ ἆθλον ἄν σοι τοῦδ ἔτ [*] ἀλγίω πόροι .
sections.translation: But he might inflict on you an ordeal even more bitter than this .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ὁ δ οὖν ποιείτω : πάντα προσδοκητά μοι .
sections.translation: Let him , for all I care ! I am prepared for anything .

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: οἱ προσκυνοῦντες τὴν Ἀδράστειαν σοφοί .
sections.translation: Wise are they who do homage to Necessity . [*]

sections.label: Section 937
sections.original: σέβου , προσεύχου , θῶπτε [*] τὸν κρατοῦντ ἀεί .
sections.translation: Worship , adore , and fawn upon whoever is your lord . But for Zeus I care less than nothing . Let him do his will , let him hold his power

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: ὅπως θέλει : δαρὸν γὰρ οὐκ ἄρξει θεοῖς .
sections.translation: for his little day — since he will not bear sway over the gods for long . But wait , for over there I see his messenger , the servant of our new lord and master . Certainly he has come to announce some news .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: σὲ τὸν σοφιστήν , τὸν πικρῶς ὑπέρπικρον ,
sections.translation: To you , the clever and crafty , bitter beyond all bitterness ,

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: τὸν ἐξαμαρτόντ εἰς θεοὺς ἐφημέροις [*]
sections.translation: who has sinned against the gods in bestowing honors upon creatures of a day — to you , thief of fire , I speak . The Father commands that you tell what marriage you boast of , whereby he is to be hurled from power — and this , mark well , set forth in no riddling fashion ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ἀλλ αὔθ ἕκαστ ἔκφραζε : [*] μηδέ μοι διπλᾶς
sections.translation: but point by point , as the case exactly stands ; and do not impose upon me a double journey , Prometheus — you see Zeus is not appeased by dealings such as yours .

sections.label: Section 953
sections.original: σεμνόστομός γε καὶ φρονήματος πλέως
sections.translation: Bravely spoken , in truth , and swollen with pride is your speech , as befits a minion of the gods .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: νέον νέοι κρατεῖτε καὶ δοκεῖτε δὴ
sections.translation: Young you are , as young your power , and you think indeed that you inhabit heights beyond the reach of grief . Have I not seen two sovereigns cast out from these heights ? A third , the present lord , I shall live to see cast out in ruin most shameful and most swift . Do you think

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: ταρβεῖν ὑποπτήσσειν τε τοὺς νέους θεούς ;
sections.translation: I quail , perhaps , and cower before these upstart gods ? Far from it — no , not at all . But scurry back the way you came ; for you shall learn nothing about which you question me .

sections.label: Section 964
sections.original: τοιοῖσδε μέντοι καὶ πρὶν αὐθαδίσμασιν
sections.translation: Yet it was by such proud wilfulness before , too ,

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ἐς τάσδε σαυτὸν πημονὰς καθώρμισας . [*]
sections.translation: that you brought yourself to this harbor of distress .

sections.label: Section 966
sections.original: τῆς σῆς λατρείας τὴν ἐμὴν δυσπραξίαν ,
sections.translation: For your servitude , rest assured , I ’ d not barter my hard lot , not I .

sections.label: Section 968
sections.original: κρεῖσσον γὰρ οἶμαι τῇδε λατρεύειν πέτρᾳ [*]
sections.translation: Better , no doubt , to serve this rock than be the trusted messenger of Father Zeus !

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: οὕτως ὑβρίζειν τοὺς ὑβρίζοντας χρεών .
sections.translation: Such is the proper style for the insolent to offer insult .

sections.label: Section 971
sections.original: χλιδᾶν ἔοικας τοῖς παροῦσι πράγμασι .
sections.translation: I think you revel in your present plight .

sections.label: Section 972
sections.original: χλιδῶ ; χλιδῶντας ὧδε τοὺς ἐμοὺς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: I revel ? Oh , I wish that I might see my enemies revelling in this way ! And you , too , I count among them .

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: ἦ κἀμὲ γάρ τι συμφοραῖς ἐπαιτιᾷ ;
sections.translation: What ! You blame me in some way for your calamities ?

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: ἁπλῷ λόγῳ τοὺς πάντας ἐχθαίρω θεούς ,
sections.translation: In one word , I hate all the gods that received good at my hands and with ill requite me wrongfully .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: κλύω σ ἐγὼ μεμηνότ οὐ σμικρὰν [*] νόσον .
sections.translation: Your words declare you stricken with no slight madness .

sections.label: Section 978
sections.original: νοσοῖμ ἄν , εἰ νόσημα τοὺς ἐχθροὺς στυγεῖν .
sections.translation: Mad I may be — if it is madness to loathe one ’ s enemies .

sections.label: Section 979
sections.original: εἴης φορητὸς οὐκ ἄν , εἰ πράσσοις καλῶς .
sections.translation: You would be unbearable if you were prosperous .

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: τόδε Ζεὺς τοὔπος οὐκ ἐπίσταται .
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐκδιδάσκει πάνθ ὁ γηράσκων χρόνος .
sections.translation: But ever-ageing Time teaches all things .

sections.label: Section 982
sections.original: καὶ μὴν σύ γ οὔπω σωφρονεῖν ἐπίστασαι .
sections.translation: Yes , but you at least have not yet learned to keep a sober mind .

sections.label: Section 983
sections.original: σὲ γὰρ προσηύδων οὐκ ἂν ὄνθ ὑπηρέτην .
sections.translation: Or else I would not have addressed you , an underling .

sections.label: Section 984
sections.original: ἐρεῖν ἔοικας οὐδὲν ὧν χρῄζει πατήρ .
sections.translation: It seems you will answer nothing that the Father demands .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὀφείλων γ ἂν τίνοιμ αὐτῷ χάριν .
sections.translation: Yes , truly , I am his debtor and I should repay favor to him .

sections.label: Section 986
sections.original: ἐκερτόμησας δῆθεν ὥστε παῖδά με . [*]
sections.translation: You taunt me as though , indeed , I were a child .
